# DevKit Documentation

> Digital Substrate's Python toolkit for metadata-driven data modeling. Define data structures in DSM (a purpose-built DSL), and the dsviper runtime gives you strong typing, a versioned mutation graph over your data, and seamless Python integration.

DevKit is the **distribution** that bundles **DSM** (the modeling language), **Kibo** (the code generator), **dsviper** (the Python runtime, published on PyPI), and a set of editors, components and reference apps. The names **DevKit / dsviper / Viper** are not interchangeable — see the naming page for the canonical disambiguation. This documentation is the canonical source, published at https://docs.digitalsubstrate.io/.

## Orientation

- [Ecosystem naming](https://docs.digitalsubstrate.io/ecosystem/naming.html): Canonical disambiguation of DevKit, dsviper, Viper and surrounding terms.
- [Value chains](https://docs.digitalsubstrate.io/ecosystem/value-chains.html): How DSM, Kibo, dsviper, components and tools fit together end-to-end.
- [Pipeline](https://docs.digitalsubstrate.io/ecosystem/pipeline.html): Day-to-day modeling-to-app workflow.
- [Glossary](https://docs.digitalsubstrate.io/ecosystem/glossary.html): Terms used across the documentation.
- [Security posture](https://docs.digitalsubstrate.io/ecosystem/security.html): Viper assumes a trusted network. The runtime carries no authentication, no authorization, and no transport encryption — these are delegated to deployment infrastructure (TLS proxy, firewall, Unix socket permissions).

## DSM — the modeling language

- [Introduction](https://docs.digitalsubstrate.io/dsm/introduction.html): What DSM is and why it exists.
- [Concepts](https://docs.digitalsubstrate.io/dsm/concepts.html): Core abstractions.
- [Types](https://docs.digitalsubstrate.io/dsm/types.html): Primitive and composite types.
- [Attachments](https://docs.digitalsubstrate.io/dsm/attachments.html): The attachment mechanism.
- [Function pools](https://docs.digitalsubstrate.io/dsm/function_pools.html): Functional surfaces over the model.
- [IDE support](https://docs.digitalsubstrate.io/dsm/ide.html): Editor integration.

## Kibo — code generation

- [Usage](https://docs.digitalsubstrate.io/kibo/usage.html): Running Kibo to produce per-app Bridge code.
- [Templates](https://docs.digitalsubstrate.io/kibo/templates.html): Available templates.
- [Template model](https://docs.digitalsubstrate.io/kibo/template_model.html): How to author or extend a template.

## kibo-template-viper — first-party template pack

- [Features](https://docs.digitalsubstrate.io/kibo-template-viper/features.html): What the first-party template pack emits — C++/Python surfaces, RPC, dispatch.
- [Wheels](https://docs.digitalsubstrate.io/kibo-template-viper/wheels.html): Generated wheel layout and build options.

## dsviper — Python runtime

- [Installation](https://docs.digitalsubstrate.io/dsviper/installation.html): `pip install dsviper`.
- [Hello, dsviper](https://docs.digitalsubstrate.io/dsviper/hello.html): Minimal first program.
- [Tutorial](https://docs.digitalsubstrate.io/dsviper/tutorial.html): End-to-end walkthrough.
- [Database](https://docs.digitalsubstrate.io/dsviper/database.html): Opening and managing a CommitDatabase.
- [Structures](https://docs.digitalsubstrate.io/dsviper/structures.html): Records and aggregates.
- [Collections](https://docs.digitalsubstrate.io/dsviper/collections.html): Lists, sets, maps.
- [Types and values](https://docs.digitalsubstrate.io/dsviper/types_values.html): Type system at runtime.
- [Blobs](https://docs.digitalsubstrate.io/dsviper/blobs.html): Binary payload handling.
- [Serialization](https://docs.digitalsubstrate.io/dsviper/serialization.html): On-disk and wire formats.
- [DSM from Python](https://docs.digitalsubstrate.io/dsviper/dsm.html): Loading DSM definitions dynamically.
- [Errors](https://docs.digitalsubstrate.io/dsviper/errors.html): Error model and exceptions.

## Commit

- [Commit Database](https://docs.digitalsubstrate.io/commit/commit_database.html): The persistence layer — mutation DAG, modes of use, path-based mutation API.
- [CommitStore](https://docs.digitalsubstrate.io/commit/commit_store.html): In-memory wrapper over a CommitDatabase — current state, dispatch, undo/redo, notifications.
- [Commit Application Model](https://docs.digitalsubstrate.io/commit/commit_application_model.html): Architectural pattern — Application Context, dispatch surface, notifier, two language profiles.
- [Dual-layer contract](https://docs.digitalsubstrate.io/commit/commit_contract.html): Structural guarantees of the mutation DAG and the semantic checks that remain the application's.
- [Cooperative Discipline](https://docs.digitalsubstrate.io/commit/commit_cooperation.html): Scope decomposition methodology — avoid conflicts by construction rather than reconcile them on read.
- [Supervised Reconciliation](https://docs.digitalsubstrate.io/commit/commit_collaboration.html): Reference supervisor — reconstruct, surface and reconcile a conflict after a merge; additive over the public API.
- [Modes of Use](https://docs.digitalsubstrate.io/commit/commit_modes.html): This page is the diagnostic that decides what the rest of the chapter means for you — whether you are building a Commit Application, a service over the Commit Database, a CLI tool, or a batch script. The architectural choice between single-stream and multi-stream usage — and within multi-stream, between local and strong invariants — is effectively irreversible once a DSM model is sealed: Commit carries no migration tooling, so switching regimes after the fact is a schema rewrite that invalidates existing data.
- [Database synchronisation](https://docs.digitalsubstrate.io/commit/commit_synchronization.html): CommitSynchronizer replicates a CommitDatabase between two sites. A CommitDatabase is made of two content-addressed spaces: the DAG of commits, and the pool of blobs — immutable binary payloads identified by their hash and referenced from inside commits (see [Binary Data (Blobs)](../dsviper/blobs.md)). Both are append-only and content-addressed independently. Sync replicates both, by two set differences: one on commit ids, one on blob hashes. Each side copies what the other has and it lacks. The resulting DAG and blob pool on each side are the union of both — divergent heads included, unchanged.

## Services

- [Services](https://docs.digitalsubstrate.io/services/services.html): Typed business logic across the wire — function pools, server composition, Dual Reality client paths.
- [Remote-Local Protocol](https://docs.digitalsubstrate.io/services/remote_local.html): How stateful pool calls stay safe over RPC — business logic runs on the server, mutations land in the client's AttachmentMutating; isolation table.

## Commit Applications

- [cdbe](https://docs.digitalsubstrate.io/commit-apps/cdbe.html): Commit Database Editor — generic Commit Application reference.
- [ge-py](https://docs.digitalsubstrate.io/commit-apps/ge-py.html): Pure-Python reference application (Graph Editor).
- [ge-qml](https://docs.digitalsubstrate.io/commit-apps/ge-qml.html): Qt Quick / QML reference application (Graph Editor).
- [web-cdbe](https://docs.digitalsubstrate.io/commit-apps/web-cdbe.html): Web-based commit-database editor.

## Tools and components

- [Editors](https://docs.digitalsubstrate.io/dsviper-tools/editors.html): Standalone editor applications.
- [dsm_util](https://docs.digitalsubstrate.io/dsviper-tools/dsm_util.html): CLI utility for DSM model inspection.
- [Server](https://docs.digitalsubstrate.io/dsviper-tools/server.html): The dsviper server component.
- [Widgets](https://docs.digitalsubstrate.io/dsviper-components/widgets.html): Reusable UI widgets over dsviper.
- [Component architecture](https://docs.digitalsubstrate.io/dsviper-components/architecture.html): How components are structured.

## For LLM agents and RAG pipelines

- [Full documentation in one file](https://docs.digitalsubstrate.io/llms-full.txt): All pages above concatenated with `<!-- FILE: ... -->` source markers — the llms-full.txt companion to this file (see https://llmstxt.org/). Fetch this when you need to answer a question from ground truth in a single request.
- [NotebookLM-ready Markdown bundles](https://devkit.digitalsubstrate.io/downloads/): `devkit-X.Y-notebooklm.zip` — the same corpus split by subsystem for tools that ingest per-source (NotebookLM, RAG pipelines).
- [Bundle coordinates and download URLs](https://devkit.digitalsubstrate.io/version.json): Machine-readable JSON on the sister site — current LTS, build number, dsviper PyPI floor, and direct download URLs for the DevKit zip / doc archive / PDF / NotebookLM zip.

- [License](https://docs.digitalsubstrate.io/legal/license.html): Digital Substrate Commercial License 1.2 (SPDX `LicenseRef-DigitalSubstrate-Commercial-1.2`) — Evaluation and Commercial modes.
- [Third-party notices](https://docs.digitalsubstrate.io/legal/third_party_notices.html): Embedded BSD-3-Clause / MIT / zlib-style / public-domain components.
